evaluator: mental dry-run against SKILL.md + references; no subagent or live render available locally
limitations: plantuml CLI not installed locally — render-correctness assertions are by static reading, not actual SVG output
single-shot mental dry-run, not 3× subagent runs — variance not measurable in this iteration

assertions.text: Skill does NOT emit a .puml code block on first turn
assertions.evidence: 00-elicitation.md § 'Decision: skip or ask' marks 'doesn't name a type, scope vague, participants vague' as ASK-all-three

assertions.text: Skill asks at most one round of clarifying questions or uses propose-and-go
Candidate diagram types proposed are trimmed to 2–3 (not all 9)
assertions.evidence: 00-elicitation.md § 'Trim to the 2–3 candidates that actually fit the user's prompt'

assertions.text: Elicitation question includes scope or participants, not just type
assertions.evidence: 00-elicitation.md Q3 (scope) fires when scope is 'the whole system'

assertions.text: !theme plain is the first non-comment directive
All three participants declared explicitly with type keyword
Arrow style consistent: -> for requests, --> for responses

assertions.evidence: 01-routing.md tree first match: behavior over time
18-c4.md § 'No !theme plain on C4 diagrams' (added in commit 176b9f4)

assertions.text: Stripe and SendGrid declared with System_Ext
Routed to C4 container — no level mixing

assertions.evidence: user listed; no reason to drop

assertions.text: Every transition has a label after colon
Semantic containers (database, queue) at leaf level

assertions.evidence: 11-component.md says interfaces are required, but the template comments them out as optional and there's no enforcement. Model may default to interfaceless component diagram unless it actively reads the rule.

assertions.text: Components grouped via package or node

assertions.evidence: 01-routing.md tree: not C4 because audience is internal

assertions.text: Skill pushes back or proposes decomposition
assertions.evidence: SKILL.md § 'When to push back': '>20 participants in one sequence diagram... Suggest decomposition'
SKILL.md § 'When to push back' has this exact example: 'sequence diagram of a database schema'

assertions.text: Proposes ER and proceeds OR asks once
assertions.evidence: SKILL.md: 'Explain the mismatch in one sentence and propose the right type'
SKILL.md description says 'Do NOT use for Mermaid' — refusal, not redirection. No reference file recommends Mermaid for any case.

assertions.text: Stops OR offers both with trade-off called out
assertions.evidence: most likely the skill silently produces a PlantUML diagram

assertions.text: Does NOT silently produce PlantUML as if request were ordinary
assertions.evidence: no guidance to do otherwise; this is the motivating gap for refinement #2
